rulings.Case Title: ALI AHMAD, MD VS. MEDICAL BOARD OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Tuesday, January 21, 2025, line 9, PETITIONER ALI AHMAD, MD'S Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate Continued to February 6, 2025. The opposing party shall provide a courtesy copy of the opposition per SF Local Rule 2.7B with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM Y. MOORES M.D VS. MEDICAL BOARD OF CALIFORNIA, A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Second Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On Peremptory Writ
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Tuesday, January 21, 2025, line 10. PETITIONER WILLIAM MOORES , AN INDIVIDUAL Second Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On Peremptory Writ *THE COURT'S FULL AND COMPLETE TENTATIVE RULING HAS BEEN EMAILED TO COUNSEL* Petitioner William Y. Moores' petition for writ of mandate is denied. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: VIA SENTI, A CALIFORNIA 501(C)(3) NONPROFIT VS. CARRIE ALISON MALONEY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions For Failure To Comply With Court'S Discovery Order, Including Terminating And Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, January 21, 2025, line 3, PLAINTIFF VIA SENTI, A CALIFORNIA 501(C)(3) NONPROFIT , DBA HEIRS TO OUR OCEAN's Motion For Sanctions For Failure To Comply With Court'S Discovery Order, Including Terminating And Monetary Sanctions; Plaintiff Via Senti's Motion For Sanctions For Failure To Comply With Court's Discovery Order, Including Terminating And Monetary Sanctions is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. Defendant, through counsel, violated the court's September 20, 2024, and their misconduct is not excused. Defendant's actions in connection with the discovery requests, order and responses at issue were not substantially justified. Defendant, shall pay $4,000.00 to Plaintiff in sanctions within 10 days of notice of this order, proof of payment to be filed with the court in the register of actions within 3 courts days of payment. As in the September 20, 2024 order, the court imposes sanctions on Defendant. If Defense counsel believes themselves responsible, the court trusts Defendant and counsel can work that out. The request for terminating sanctions is denied. The record does not support imposition if the most drastic sanctions available at this time. To the extent Plaintiff seeks additional relief, the motion is denied. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. rulings.Case Title: KIERAN FARR VS. CHRISTIAN ISAM BABA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Responses To Defendants Discovery Requests
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, January 21, 2025, line 4, DEFENDANT CHRISTIAN BABA's Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Responses To Defendants Discovery Requests; Before the court is Defendant Christian Isam Baba's Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Responses To Defendants Discovery Requests to which no opposition has yet been filed. In light of Plaintiff's counsel's recent entry into the case, the court continues this motion to February 27, 2025, at 9:00 am in this department. The court orders counsel to meet and confer in person, by telephone or by videoconference as soon as practical but in no event later than January 27, 2025 close of business. COUnsel shall try to resolve issues regarding discovery responses and sanctions Any opposition to this motion must be filed no later than February 111, 2025. Any reply to that opposition must be filed no later than February 18, 2025. Hard copy courtesy copies must be delivered to this department within 24 hours of the filing of the papers. Moving Party is advised that stacked discovery motions are disfavored. Stacked motions may be ordered off calendar in favor of individual motions focused each on a single discovery vehicle (e.g., Frog Set, SRog Set, RFP Set, RFA Set, deposition notice) addressed to a single party. (Notably, though, if a stacked motion is ever appropriate it is where, as here, the responding party has provided no response at all to discovery requests served together at the same time.) The parties are advised if discovery disputes persist, the court, at the parties' expense, may appoint a discovery referee. The court may continue the next discovery motion to give the parties time to seek appointment of a referee. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/JMQ)
